findings.detail: The planner can instruct users to delete more than the classified tool artifacts. When every known `scripts/req/*` kit file is byte-identical, `renderRevertSection` collapses the removal plan to `rm -rf scripts/req` (line 436). The planner never scans the target `scripts/req` directory for extra user files, so a repo containing `scripts/req/local-helper.ts` or notes would receive a command that deletes unclassified user content. This violates the safety model: only known removable files should be listed, or unknown extra files must be detected and force the directory-wide command off. Add a regression test with an extra target-only file under `scripts/req/`.

next_action: Replace the directory-wide `rm -rf scripts/req` suggestion with per-file removal or guard it by proving the target directory contains only known kit files; add the extra-file regression, then rerun typecheck/tests/smoke.

observations.detail: `npm run typecheck` completed with exit 0. Targeted vitest execution could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `vitest.config.ts.timestamp-*.mjs` under the workspace and hit EPERM, so I could not use local test results as approval evidence.
